answer:
**Charles Dunoyer's classification of the diverse *ordres de travaux et de fonctions* (orders of labors and functions) in industrial society appears in Chapter 13 ("III. Des divers ordres de travaux et de fonctions qu'embrasse la société industrielle") of *Nouveau traité d'économie sociale, Vol. II*.**[^1]

Dunoyer rejects hierarchical rankings among professions, likening them to interdependent organs in a body: "there is, among them, neither first nor last; that they are in a mutual dependence; that each has need of all the others; that all contribute to the life of each."[^2] He insists no profession is inherently superior; subordination exists only within specific enterprises (e.g., workers to managers), not between broad classes.[^3]

The **principle** of classification distinguishes professions by their *object* (always man, as the end) and *subject* (direct target of action): things (indirect service to man) versus persons (direct action on man). All produce *utility* if exercised justly (excluding predatory acts like privilege or violence, which destroy utility).[^4]

### Main Categories
1. **Industries that appropriate things to the needs of man** (acting on physical nature/material objects, e.g., agriculture, manufacturing). These fix utilities in matter.[^5]
2. **Industries exercised directly upon man** (acting on persons). These modify human faculties, producing "immaterial" utilities like health, skills, knowledge, and virtues—durable, accumulable, and exchangeable, contra economists like Smith and Say.[^6] Dunoyer further describes:
   | Sub-aspect of human nature | Examples |
   |----------------------------|----------|
   | Prepare for exploiting the material world | (Implied: physical training, strength/agility via gymnastics, fencing masters)[^7] |
   | Cultivate physical nature | Doctors, gymnastics masters (health, vigor)[^7] |
   | Educate intelligence/imagination | Teachers, scientists, artists (knowledge, taste, soul)[^7] |
   | Care for affective faculties | Moralists, priests, magistrates (morality, justice, habits)[^7] |

### Additional Functions (Non-Industries)
Beyond labors, society requires *functions* all professions perform: **associating** (forming partnerships), **exchanging** (buying/selling utilities), and **bequeathing** (inheritance). These enable industry but are not productive acts themselves.[^8]

Dunoyer defines *industrial society* as all honest professions minus injustices (e.g., monopolies, predation), excluding privileged orders.[^9] Later chapters apply liberty conditions to specifics (e.g., agriculture in Ch. 17, arts on men in Ch. 18–19).[^10]

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §7](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^3]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §7](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^4]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §8](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-50babc); [§5](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-fb8d6b).

[^5]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §8](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-50babc); [§3](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-e26e62).

[^6]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §§4–6](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-3efbf3).

[^7]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §7](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^8]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §8](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-50babc).

[^9]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, [Chapter 13 §6](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-f06e85).

[^10]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, Chapters 15–19 (via metadata).
